text: Meeting deadlines can be stressful, but good time management helps. Start by breaking your project into smaller tasks and estimating how long each will take. Prioritization is key—focus on the most important or time-sensitive work first, and set realistic goals for each day. Scope management is also important: if the project keeps expanding, you risk falling behind. Learn to say "no" to new requests that don't fit your plan. Finally, communicate early with your team or manager if you anticipate a delay. Explain the issue and propose a solution, like adjusting the deadline or getting extra help. Clear communication builds trust and prevents last-minute surprises.
